meta.source: Hana's Directive — 2026-02-24 (FULL REWRITE: Phases 0–8, Claude Code integration, TUI muxing, MCP deepening, integration tests)
meta.maintenance: Update status IMMEDIATELY after every sub-task. Deviations logged within plan. NO priorities. NO estimates. ALL tasks MUST be completed.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts: UTMOST GOAL: pr-split MUST work end-to-end with Claude Code integration, PROVEN by integration tests
Rule of Two: NO commits until TWO contiguous, issue-free review cycles
ALL checks MUST pass on ALL platforms (macOS, Linux via Docker, Windows via moo)
Use 'make' for builds — pipe long outputs through 'tail' or redirect to log files
NO dead code: deadcode checker must pass. Wire it up or delete it. Never add to .deadcodeignore
NO AI slop: every line must have a reason. Every feature must be validated. Every export must be tested
Two TUI paradigms: go-prompt (line-based, TUIManager) and BubbleTea (full-screen). TerminalIO shared
Goja is single-threaded: event loop serializes JS, JSRunner for cross-goroutine calls
MCP over stdio: per-instance config, Claude Code spawns as child with --mcp-config flag
mandatoryDirectives: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start — trust NO chat history
Update blueprint.json status after EVERY sub-task
Die coding — work until the very last token
If lost: READ DIRECTIVE.txt → THINK → ACT

sequentialTasks.task: T050: Design TUI mux architecture — document in architecture-claude-mux.md
sequentialTasks.description: New section 11 in architecture-claude-mux.md. Command-blocking model, forwarding architecture diagram, state management, status bar design, Go/JS API.

sequentialTasks.task: T051: Implement TUIMux type in internal/termui/mux
sequentialTasks.description: Created internal/termui/mux/mux.go (364 lines). TUIMux struct with mutex, stdin/stdout/termFd, child (io.ReadWriteCloser), active Side, toggleKey, resizeFn, statusEnabled, claudeStatus, passthroughActive. New() constructor, Attach/Detach/ActiveSide methods. Thread-safe.

sequentialTasks.task: T052: Implement PTY passthrough mode in TUIMux
sequentialTasks.description: RunPassthrough(ctx) method in mux.go. Two goroutines: stdin→child with toggle key interception, child→stdout. 4096-byte buffers. Returns ExitReason (ExitToggle/ExitChildExit/ExitContext/ExitError). Proper context cancellation.

sequentialTasks.task: T053: Implement terminal state save/restore in TUIMux
sequentialTasks.description: RunPassthrough saves terminal state via term.MakeRaw(termFd), defers term.Restore. termFd=-1 skips (for tests). Clean save/restore on all exit paths.

sequentialTasks.task: T054: Implement status bar overlay for TUI mux
sequentialTasks.description: setScrollRegion/resetScrollRegion/renderStatusBar in mux.go. ANSI scroll region restricts output to rows 1..(h-1), last row shows '[Claude] status │ Ctrl+] to switch' in reverse video. SetClaudeStatus/SetStatusEnabled config methods.

sequentialTasks.task: T055: Expose TUIMux to JavaScript via engine globals
sequentialTasks.description: Created TUIMux in pr_split.go Execute(). Exposed as 'tuiMux' global with attach/detach/switchToClaude/isClaudeActive/setStatus/setToggleKey/setStatusEnabled/setResizeFunc. Uses os.Stdin/Stdout directly (command-blocking model). WrapStringIO adapter in mux.go for AgentHandle bridging. All linters pass (vet, staticcheck, deadcode, betteralign).

sequentialTasks.task: T056: Add TUI command 'claude' to pr-split — toggle to Claude TUI
sequentialTasks.description: Added 'claude' and 'claude-status' commands in pr_split_script.js buildCommands(). claude: lazy-creates ClaudeCodeExecutor, supports 'claude spawn' to resolve+spawn, 'claude' to attach+switchToClaude via tuiMux, auto-detach on return. claude-status: shows command/session/process/mux state. Help updated with both commands. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T057: Handle edge cases in TUI mux — Claude exits while muxed
sequentialTasks.description: Handled by RunPassthrough returning ExitChildExit on child EOF. JS 'claude' command prints 'Back to osm. (reason: child-exit)' and auto-detaches. TestRunPassthrough_ChildExit verifies this path.

sequentialTasks.task: T058: Handle edge cases in TUI mux — terminal resize
sequentialTasks.description: SetResizeFunc exposed in JS bridge (tuiMux.setResizeFunc). The callback receives (rows, cols) and can call pty.Resize(). Status bar re-render on resize is implicit in scroll region architecture. Full SIGWINCH integration deferred to Phase 6 (cross-platform concerns).

sequentialTasks.task: T059: Handle edge cases in TUI mux — osm commands while Claude is active
sequentialTasks.description: Command-blocking model: osm cannot receive commands while Claude is active (by design). MCP event monitoring happens via background Go goroutines independent of TUI ownership. tuiMux.setStatus() allows updating status bar from any goroutine. Background monitoring wired when MCP transport is connected (Phase 4).

sequentialTasks.task: T060: Write unit tests for TUIMux
sequentialTasks.description: 14 tests in mux_test.go. ALL PASS with -race. Tests: New, AttachDetach, NoChild, ToggleKey, ChildExit, ContextCancel, ChildOutput, DoubleCallFails, DetachDuringPassthroughFails, SetToggleKey, ToggleKeyInMiddleOfData, ExitReasonString, SetClaudeStatus, RenderStatusBar. mockChild + blockingReader mocks.

sequentialTasks.description: make lint PASS (vet+staticcheck+betteralign+deadcode). make build PASS. 15 mux tests + 42 pr_split tests PASS with -race. Zero errors.

sequentialTasks.description: Committed ea04bd2. Rule of Two: 2/2 PASS. 5 files changed, +1174/-1.

sequentialTasks.description: CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE implemented in pr_split_script.js with Go text/template. Variables: Files, FileStatuses, ModulePath, Language. Tested: template content + render output. 20 tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T064: Design the split-plan prompt for Claude Code
sequentialTasks.description: SPLIT_PLAN_PROMPT_TEMPLATE implemented. Variables: Classification, MaxFilesPerSplit, BranchPrefix, StackingMode. Tested.

sequentialTasks.task: T065: Design the conflict-resolution prompt for Claude Code
sequentialTasks.description: CONFLICT_RESOLUTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE implemented. Variables: BranchName, ErrorOutput, ExitCode, Files, GoModContent. Tested.

sequentialTasks.task: T066: Implement automated split flow — Step 1: Analyze diff
sequentialTasks.description: automatedSplit() step 1: calls analyzeDiff(), early return on error/no changes. Tested via AutoSplitFallsBackToHeuristic.

sequentialTasks.task: T067: Implement automated split flow — Step 2: Spawn Claude Code
sequentialTasks.description: Step 2: ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn() with MCP instance. Falls back to heuristic on failure. Tested: nonexistent command → heuristic fallback.

sequentialTasks.task: T068: Implement automated split flow — Step 3: Send classification request
sequentialTasks.description: Step 3: renders classification prompt via template, sends to Claude stdin. Polls for result file with configurable timeout.

sequentialTasks.task: T069: Implement automated split flow — Step 4: Receive classification
sequentialTasks.description: Step 4: pollForFile for classification result. Parses JSON, validates all files classified, converts via classificationToGroups(). Tested: classificationToGroups round-trip.

sequentialTasks.task: T070: Implement automated split flow — Step 5: Generate and validate plan
sequentialTasks.description: Step 5: polls for split plan, validates, falls back to local createSplitPlan() from classification groups if invalid.

sequentialTasks.task: T071: Implement automated split flow — Step 6: Execute plan
sequentialTasks.description: Step 6: calls executeSplit() + verifyEquivalence(). Reuses heuristic path.

sequentialTasks.task: T072: Implement automated split flow — Step 7: Verify each split
sequentialTasks.description: Step 7: verifySplits() with failure collection for conflict resolution.

sequentialTasks.task: T073: Implement automated split flow — Step 8: Claude-assisted conflict resolution
sequentialTasks.description: Step 8: resolveConflictsWithClaude() — prompt render, send, poll, apply patches, re-verify loop. Falls back to local strategies.

sequentialTasks.task: T074: Implement automated split flow — Step 9: Re-split fallback
sequentialTasks.description: Step 9: re-split path in automatedSplit — sends re-classification request with conflict constraints, max 1 re-split.

sequentialTasks.task: T075: Implement automated split flow — Step 10: Completion and reporting
sequentialTasks.description: Step 10: final report with splits, conflicts, Claude interactions, JSON output option. ClaudeCodeExecutor.close() cleanup.

sequentialTasks.description: assessIndependence() + splitsAreIndependent(): dir overlap + Go import overlap detection. Tested: NoOverlap, WithOverlap, Singles.

sequentialTasks.description: 'auto-split' TUI command calls automatedSplit(). Tested: HelpIncludesAutoSplit, AutoSplitFallsBackToHeuristic.

sequentialTasks.description: 'run --mode auto|heuristic' flag. Default heuristic. 'set mode' command. Tested: SetModeCommand, RunModeAutoFallback, RunModeHeuristicExplicit.

sequentialTasks.task: T079: Implement classification prompt template with Go text/template
sequentialTasks.description: CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE with renderClassificationPrompt(). Uses osm:text/template. Tested: RenderClassificationPrompt.

sequentialTasks.task: T080: Implement split-plan prompt template
sequentialTasks.description: SPLIT_PLAN_PROMPT_TEMPLATE with renderSplitPlanPrompt(). Tested: RenderSplitPlanPrompt.

sequentialTasks.description: CONFLICT_RESOLUTION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE with renderConflictPrompt(). Tested: RenderConflictPrompt.

sequentialTasks.task: T082: Run full build verification after automated pipeline
sequentialTasks.description: make (build+lint+test) ALL GREEN. 44 packages pass. vet, staticcheck, deadcode clean. 20 Phase 4 tests pass with -race.

sequentialTasks.task: T083: Commit Phase 4 automated splitting pipeline
sequentialTasks.description: Committed 8f42e0b. Rule of Two: 2/2 PASS. 3 files changed, +2217/-108. Bug fixes: fileStatuses, .passed, .results, extractGoImports, log.printf.

sequentialTasks.task: T084: Extend AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES with additional strategies
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split_script.js, add new auto-fix strategies beyond go-mod-tidy and go-generate-sum: (1) 'go-build-missing-imports': detect missing import errors, add required imports, (2) 'npm-install': for JS/TS projects, run 'npm install', (3) 'make-generate': run 'make generate' or 'go generate ./...', (4) 'add-missing-files': detect references to files not in the split, add them from source branch. Each strategy follows the existing {name, detect, fix} pattern.

sequentialTasks.task: T085: Implement Claude-assisted fix strategy
sequentialTasks.description: Add a strategy 'claude-fix' to AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES. This strategy: (1) detect: always returns true if Claude is available, (2) fix: sends the verification error to Claude via reportConflict MCP tool, waits for reportResolution, applies the suggested patches. This integrates Claude into the existing resolveConflicts() flow, making it available to both the 'fix' TUI command and the automated pipeline.

sequentialTasks.task: T086: Implement verification loop with retry budget
sequentialTasks.description: Modify resolveConflicts() to accept a retryBudget config (default 3). For each failed branch: try AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES in order. If the Claude strategy is used, it counts as one retry per attempt. Track total retries across all branches. If budget exceeded, stop and report remaining failures. Add retryBudget to runtime config and 'set' command.

sequentialTasks.task: T087: Implement re-split fallback in resolveConflicts
sequentialTasks.description: Add a fallback mode to resolveConflicts(): if all strategies fail for a branch, return a 'reSplitNeeded' flag with the problematic files. The caller (automated pipeline or TUI) can then trigger re-classification. The re-split should constrain Claude to separate the conflicting files into different groups.

sequentialTasks.task: T088: Write unit tests for new auto-fix strategies
sequentialTasks.description: In pr_split_test.go, add tests for each new strategy: (1) go-build-missing-imports detects and fixes import errors, (2) npm-install detects package.json and runs npm, (3) make-generate runs generation, (4) add-missing-files detects and adds files. Use mock exec and git helpers.

sequentialTasks.task: T089: Write unit tests for Claude-assisted fix strategy
sequentialTasks.description: Test the claude-fix strategy with mock MCP responses: (1) Claude proposes valid fix → verify accepted and applied, (2) Claude proposes invalid fix → verify rejected, (3) Claude times out → verify fallback to next strategy, (4) Multiple retries until budget exhausted.

sequentialTasks.task: T090: Run full build verification after conflict resolution enhancement
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make' and 'go test -race ./...'. All pass. This is the Phase 5 gate.

sequentialTasks.task: T091: Commit Phase 5 conflict resolution
sequentialTasks.description: Stage all Phase 5 changes. Rule of Two. Commit: 'feat(pr-split): enhanced conflict resolution — Claude-assisted fixes, retry budget, re-split fallback'. Push.

sequentialTasks.task: T092: Create mock Claude provider for testing
sequentialTasks.description: Create a new type MockClaudeProvider in internal/builtin/claudemux/ (or internal/testutil/) that implements the Provider interface. It simulates Claude Code behavior: (1) Spawn() returns a MockAgentHandle that queues predefined responses, (2) Responses can be configured per-test: classification responses, plan responses, conflict resolution responses, (3) Tracks all prompts received (for assertion). (4) Simulates MCP tool calls by writing to a result directory. This mock enables full-pipeline testing without a real Claude installation.

sequentialTasks.task: T093: Create mock MCP transport for testing
sequentialTasks.description: Create a MockMCPTransport that wraps the InMemoryTransport from the MCP SDK. It should: (1) Allow injecting predefined tool call responses, (2) Capture all tool calls made by the test, (3) Simulate delays and timeouts, (4) Simulate MCP errors. This enables testing the full MCP protocol without a real server process.

sequentialTasks.task: T094: Create test harness for full-pipeline testing
sequentialTasks.description: Create a TestPipeline helper in internal/command/pr_split_test.go (or a new file). It sets up: (1) Temp git repo with configurable initial files, (2) Feature branch with configurable changes, (3) MockClaudeProvider configured with expected responses, (4) Engine with pr_split_script.js loaded, (5) prSplitConfig with appropriate config. The helper returns an object that can run commands and assert outputs. This is the foundation for all Phase 6 tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T095: Integration test — Scenario 1: Feature branch with 20+ files across 5 packages
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_LargeFeatureBranch. Setup: git repo with 5 Go packages (cmd/app, internal/api, internal/db, internal/auth, pkg/client), each with 4-5 files changed (additions, modifications, deletions). Mock Claude classifies into 5 groups matching packages. Assert: (1) 5 split branches created, (2) Each branch contains only correct package files, (3) Tree hash equivalence passes, (4) Branch stacking is correct (each based on previous), (5) Commit messages reference package names.

sequentialTasks.task: T096: Integration test — Scenario 2: Refactoring with renames, moves, deletions
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_RefactoringBranch. Setup: git repo where feature branch has: 3 file renames (R status), 2 file deletions (D status), 5 file modifications (M status), 3 new files (A status). Mock Claude classifies into 3 groups: renames, deletions+related-modifications, new-features. Assert: (1) Renamed files handled correctly in each split, (2) Deleted files removed in correct branch, (3) New files added in correct branch, (4) Tree hash equivalence passes.

sequentialTasks.task: T097: Integration test — Scenario 3: Splits that break compilation
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_BrokenSplits. Setup: git repo with Go files that have cross-package dependencies. Mock Claude classifies into groups that break compilation (package A in split 1 needs package B in split 2). Assert: (1) Verification detects failure, (2) Conflict resolution triggers, (3) Mock Claude proposes fix (add missing dependency to go.mod or move file), (4) Fix is applied, (5) Re-verification passes after fix.

sequentialTasks.task: T098: Integration test — Scenario 4: Independent changes (non-stacked)
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_IndependentChanges. Setup: git repo with changes in completely unrelated directories (docs/, tests/, and a config file). Mock Claude classifies as independent groups. Assert: (1) independenceDetection identifies pairs as independent, (2) Report shows independence matrix, (3) Each branch can be verified independently against the base (not against the previous split), (4) Report notes these can be merged in any order.

sequentialTasks.task: T099: Integration test — Scenario 5: Heuristic fallback when Claude unavailable
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_HeuristicFallback. Setup: same as Scenario 1, but make Claude command resolution fail (no claude binary). Assert: (1) Automated mode detects Claude unavailable, (2) Falls back to heuristic mode with warning, (3) Heuristic split completes successfully, (4) No MCP operations attempted.

sequentialTasks.task: T100: Integration test — Scenario 6: Claude timeout and retry
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_ClaudeTimeout. Setup: MockClaudeProvider that delays classification response beyond timeout. Assert: (1) Timeout detected after configurable duration, (2) Retry initiated (up to configured max), (3) Second attempt succeeds (mock responds faster), (4) Final result is correct.

sequentialTasks.task: T101: Integration test — Scenario 7: Full pipeline with plan save/load
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_PlanPersistence. Setup: run automated split to generate plan. Save plan to temp file. Cleanup branches. Load plan from file. Execute from loaded plan. Assert: (1) Loaded plan matches saved plan, (2) Execution from loaded plan succeeds, (3) Tree hash equivalence passes.

sequentialTasks.task: T102: Integration test — Scenario 8: PR creation with mock gh CLI
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_PRCreation. Setup: run full pipeline to create split branches. Mock 'gh' CLI (replace with a script that records calls). Call create-prs command. Assert: (1) gh pr create called for each split with correct --base and --head, (2) First PR base is main, subsequent PRs base on previous split, (3) Draft flag passed, (4) PR body includes file list.

sequentialTasks.task: T103: Integration test — Scenario 9: TUI command sequence simulation
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_TUICommandSequence. Simulate interactive session: analyze → group → plan → preview → move file → rename split → execute → verify → equivalence. Assert each command's output is correct and state transitions are valid. Use engine.EvalString to dispatch TUI commands.

sequentialTasks.task: T104: Integration test — Scenario 10: End-to-end with real Claude Code (gated)
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_RealClaudeCode. Gated behind -integration flag AND requires claude CLI on PATH. Setup: real git repo with 10+ changed files. Spawn real Claude Code with MCP config. Send classification request. Wait for real classification response with 180s timeout. Generate plan. Execute. Verify equivalence. This is the ultimate validation that the pipeline works end-to-end. Skip if claude binary not found.

sequentialTasks.task: T105: Integration test — Scenario 11: End-to-end with Ollama wrapper (gated)
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_RealOllama. Gated behind -integration flag AND requires ollama CLI. Similar to T104 but uses OllamaProvider. Tests the 'ollama launch claude' path including model navigation via PTY. Assert: (1) Model selection menu detected, (2) Navigation keystrokes sent, (3) Classification received via MCP, (4) Full pipeline completes.

sequentialTasks.task: T106: Cross-platform test verification — ensure tests skip gracefully
sequentialTasks.description: For all new integration tests: verify they skip gracefully on platforms where prerequisites are missing. (1) On Windows, PTY tests should skip with clear message. (2) On CI without Claude, -integration tests skip. (3) On CI without ollama, Ollama tests skip. Ensure no test panics or hangs due to missing binaries.

sequentialTasks.task: T107: Run full build verification after integration tests
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make' and 'go test -race ./...'. All unit tests pass. Run 'go test -v -run Integration -integration ./...' for gated tests (where available). This is the Phase 6 gate.

sequentialTasks.task: T108: Commit Phase 6 integration tests
sequentialTasks.description: Stage all Phase 6 changes. Rule of Two. Commit: 'test(pr-split): deep integration tests — 11 scenarios covering full Claude Code pipeline'. Push.

sequentialTasks.task: T109: Update docs/architecture-claude-mux.md — add automated pipeline section
sequentialTasks.description: Add new section to architecture doc describing the automated splitting pipeline: (1) Flow diagram (analyze → spawn → classify → plan → execute → verify → resolve → report), (2) MCP tool usage at each step, (3) Fallback paths (Claude unavailable → heuristic, fix fails → re-split, re-split fails → manual). (4) Configuration knobs and their effects.

sequentialTasks.task: T110: Update docs/architecture-claude-mux.md — add TUI mux section
sequentialTasks.description: Add section describing TUI mux architecture: (1) Terminal ownership model, (2) Toggle key mechanism, (3) State save/restore, (4) Status bar overlay, (5) Background MCP monitoring. Include the ASCII diagram from T050.

sequentialTasks.task: T111: Update docs/reference/command.md — comprehensive pr-split documentation
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite the pr-split section in command.md. Include: (1) All flags (existing + new Claude flags), (2) All TUI commands (existing + auto-split, claude, claude-status), (3) Usage examples for heuristic mode, automated mode, mixed mode, (4) Configuration via config file, (5) Troubleshooting common issues.

sequentialTasks.task: T112: Update docs/reference/config.md — comprehensive pr-split config section
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite the [pr-split] section in config.md. Include all keys: base, strategy, max, prefix, verify, dry-run, claude-command, claude-args, claude-model, claude-config-dir, claude-env, retry-budget, mode (auto/heuristic). Include example configurations for different setups.

sequentialTasks.task: T113: Update docs/scripting.md — document pr-split JS API
sequentialTasks.description: Add a section to scripting.md documenting the globalThis.prSplit API: all exported functions, their parameters, return values, and usage examples. This enables advanced users to call pr-split functions from custom scripts.

sequentialTasks.task: T114: Update CHANGELOG.md — Phase 1-6 entries
sequentialTasks.description: Add comprehensive CHANGELOG entries under [Unreleased] for all phases: (1) Added: automated splitting, Claude Code integration, TUI mux, MCP protocol enhancement, new auto-fix strategies, independence detection, new TUI commands, new config keys, new flags, integration tests, (2) Changed: run command defaults to auto mode, MCP tools enhanced with bidirectional protocol, (3) Removed: any dead code removed in Phase 0.

sequentialTasks.task: T115: Update shell completion scripts for all new flags
sequentialTasks.description: In completion_command.go, add completions for: --claude-command, --claude-args, --claude-model, --claude-config-dir, --claude-env, --retry-budget, --mode. Add value completions: --mode auto|heuristic. Verify bash, zsh, and fish outputs are valid.

sequentialTasks.task: T116: Final linter sweep
sequentialTasks.description: Run all linters: go vet, staticcheck (with all checks enabled), betteralign, deadcode. Fix any issues. Ensure no new dead code was introduced. Ensure struct alignment is optimal. Ensure no ignored errors.

sequentialTasks.task: T117: Final documentation review — cross-reference all docs
sequentialTasks.description: Read all documentation files touched in this project. Verify: (1) No stale references to removed features, (2) All new features documented, (3) Architecture diagrams consistent with implementation, (4) Config reference matches code, (5) Command reference matches code. Fix any discrepancies.

sequentialTasks.task: T118: Run full build verification — final
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make' on macOS. Run 'make make-all-in-container' for Linux. Run 'make make-all-run-windows' for Windows (if available). All three must pass. This is the Phase 7 gate.

sequentialTasks.task: T119: Commit Phase 7 documentation and polish
sequentialTasks.description: Stage all Phase 7 changes. Rule of Two. Commit: 'docs(pr-split): comprehensive documentation update — architecture, commands, config, changelog'. Push.

sequentialTasks.task: T120: Scope expansion — BubbleTea split-view mode
sequentialTasks.description: Design and implement a BubbleTea-based split-view TUI for pr-split. Show osm commands in the top pane and Claude's output in the bottom pane. Use BubbleTea's Update/View pattern. This is an advanced alternative to the toggle-based mux. Users can see both TUIs simultaneously. Wire as a new mode accessible via 'set view split' TUI command.

sequentialTasks.task: T121: Scope expansion — interactive plan editor in BubbleTea
sequentialTasks.description: Implement a full-screen BubbleTea TUI for plan editing. Show splits as a list with file counts. Arrow keys to navigate, Enter to expand, d to delete, m to move files, r to rename. Replaces the text-based move/rename/merge commands with a visual editor. Wire as 'edit-plan' TUI command.

sequentialTasks.task: T122: Scope expansion — merge queue integration
sequentialTasks.description: Extend create-prs to support GitHub merge queue. After creating stacked PRs, optionally enqueue them in dependency order. Use 'gh pr merge --merge-method squash --auto' for auto-merge. Add config key pr-split.auto-merge and flag --auto-merge.

sequentialTasks.task: T123: Scope expansion — diff visualization in TUI
sequentialTasks.description: Add a 'diff' TUI command that shows a syntax-highlighted diff for a specific split. Use Lipgloss for colorization: additions in green, deletions in red, context in gray. Support paging for large diffs. Use osm:exec to run 'git diff' and parse output.

sequentialTasks.task: T124: Scope expansion — Claude conversation history persistence
sequentialTasks.description: Save Claude's classification and plan responses alongside the split plan in .pr-split-plan.json. When loading a saved plan, display Claude's rationale for each group. This helps users understand why files were grouped the way they were.

sequentialTasks.task: T125: Scope expansion — multi-Claude parallel classification
sequentialTasks.description: For very large diffs (50+ files), spawn multiple Claude instances (using Pool) to classify subsets of files in parallel. Merge classification results. Requires: (1) File list partitioning, (2) Session management per Claude instance, (3) Result merging with conflict detection (same file classified differently by different instances).

sequentialTasks.task: T126: Scope expansion — custom classification rules
sequentialTasks.description: Allow users to pre-define classification rules in config: pr-split.rules.tests=*_test.go, pr-split.rules.docs=docs/**. These rules pre-classify files before sending to Claude, reducing Claude's work and ensuring certain patterns are always grouped together. Claude can override if it explains why.

sequentialTasks.task: T127: Scope expansion — split dependency graph visualization
sequentialTasks.description: Add a 'graph' TUI command that shows the dependency graph between splits in ASCII art. Use a simple topological sort and text-based graph rendering. Highlight independent pairs. Show merge order recommendation.

sequentialTasks.task: T128: Scope expansion — performance benchmarks for split operations
sequentialTasks.description: Add benchmarks for: (1) analyzeDiff with 100/500/1000 files, (2) groupByDependency with complex import graphs, (3) createSplitPlan with 20/50 groups, (4) executeSplit with 10/20 branches, (5) MCP tool round-trip latency. Use Go testing.B and report results. Identify bottlenecks.

sequentialTasks.task: T129: Scope expansion — telemetry and analytics
sequentialTasks.description: Add optional (opt-in) telemetry for pr-split usage: (1) Number of files split, (2) Strategy used (heuristic vs auto), (3) Claude interactions count, (4) Conflict resolution success rate, (5) Total pipeline time. Store locally in .osm/telemetry/. Add 'telemetry' TUI command to view stats. Never send data externally.

sequentialTasks.task: T130: Scope expansion — plugin system for custom strategies
sequentialTasks.description: Allow users to define custom grouping strategies via JS scripts. Load from paths configured in pr-split.strategy-scripts. Each script exports a function(files, options) → groups. Register in applyStrategy switch. This enables project-specific grouping logic without modifying osm source.

sequentialTasks.task: T131: Scope expansion — retrospective analysis command
sequentialTasks.description: Add a 'retro' TUI command that analyzes a completed split: (1) Which splits were hardest to merge? (2) Which had the most conflicts? (3) Were the groups optimal? (4) Suggestions for next time. Uses saved plan data and git log analysis. Claude-assisted if available.

continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP and fix before proceeding. Zero tolerance.
continuousVerification.coveragePolicy: Every new function must have test coverage. Every export must be tested.

finalEnforcementProtocol: Rule of Two: TWO contiguous, issue-free reviews before ANY commit
ALL checks MUST pass on ALL platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)
NO dead code — deadcode must pass without additions to .deadcodeignore
If lost: READ DIRECTIVE.txt → READ blueprint.json → READ WIP.md → THINK → ACT
Die coding — the next Takumi reads WIP.md
